title: Don't Build Your Own AI Inference! It's Too Expensive & Risky! #shorts
summary: Cyril Imhof argues that building proprietary AI inference infrastructure is prohibitively expensive and risky for most organizations due to high capital requirements and financial exposure from potential strategy pivots. He recommends relying on established inference service providers instead.
writeup: This short from Cyril Imhof argues against building proprietary AI inference infrastructure, citing high capital requirements and the financial exposure that comes with potential strategy pivots. The case rests on a simple cost and risk comparison: self-built inference locks organizations into heavy upfront spending that can be stranded if priorities shift. The takeaway is to rely on established inference service providers rather than standing up the capability in-house.
